vocab.meaning: chỉnh chu, được chải chuốt cẩn thận
vocab.example: I wouldn't say I'm vain, but I do check the mirror frequently to ensure I look well-groomed before a meeting.
I usually just steal a fleeting glance at my reflection in the morning to make sure my hair isn't a total mess.

vocab.meaning: soi xét kỹ lưỡng ngoại hình
vocab.example: I’m not the type of person to stand and scrutinize my appearance for hours; I find it a bit self-absorbed.

vocab.meaning: sự chỉnh chu, ưa nhìn (để xuất hiện trước người khác)
vocab.example: For me, looking in the mirror is purely about maintaining presentability rather than an act of vanity.
Checking yourself in every reflective surface can sometimes be perceived as having narcissistic tendencies.

vocab.meaning: tự trấn an/làm cho bản thân yên tâm
vocab.example: A quick look in the mirror helps reassure myself that there’s nothing stuck in my teeth after lunch.

vocab.meaning: thứ giúp tăng cường sự tự tin
vocab.example: Seeing a sharp outfit in the reflection can serve as a real confidence booster before a big presentation.
I suppose I look in the mirror several times a day purely out of habit, especially whenever I pass through the hallway.

vocab.meaning: chăm chăm vào những khuyết điểm
vocab.example: I try not to look too often because I have a tendency to fixate on minor imperfections on my skin.

vocab.meaning: sự đánh giá bằng mắt/ngoại hình
vocab.example: I give myself a final visual appraisal in the full-length mirror right before I head out the door.
